issues.claim: Analog could not equally come from a different verse
issues.objection: Verse 4.24 has a specific argument-shape (The offering is brahman, the oblation is brahman; brahman pours into the fire of brahman; brahman al…) that the engineering analog tracks. The analog would not equally fit 4.18 (action-in-inaction), 4.7 (avatar doctrine), or 2.47 (right-to-action / fruit-detachment). The verse-specificity is observable in: (a) the central Sanskrit term carried into the engineering body, (b) the falsifiability field which forecloses misreadings specific to THIS verse, (c) the implication which is the operational shadow of THIS verse's specific claim.

notes: Force-fit test for 4.24: swap the engineering layer onto a different verse — does it still fit? No. The analog is verse-specific. PASS.
